beneficios trabajador tiempo parcial
cobertura medica empleado tiempo parcial

title.en: Health Insurance for Part-Time Workers 2026 | CoveredUSA
title.es: Seguro Medico para Trabajadores a Tiempo Parcial 2026 | CoveredUSA

description.en: Part-time workers: ACA marketplace, Medicaid, COBRA, and spouse plans. 2026 subsidy cliff explained. Find coverage even without employer benefits.
description.es: Trabajadores a tiempo parcial: mercado ACA, Medicaid, COBRA y plan del conyuge. Subsidios 2026 explicados. Encuentre cobertura sin beneficios del empleador.

h1.en: Health Insurance for Part-Time Workers in 2026
h1.es: Seguro Medico para Trabajadores a Tiempo Parcial en 2026

subhero.en: Most employers are not required to offer health insurance for part-time workers who average under 30 hours a week. In 2026, ACA Marketplace plans with income-based subsidies, Medicaid, and a spouse's employer plan are the three paths most hourly workers and variable-hours employees use to get covered.
subhero.es: La mayoria de los empleadores no estan obligados a ofrecer seguro medico a trabajadores a tiempo parcial que promedian menos de 30 horas semanales. En 2026, los planes del Mercado del ACA con subsidios, Medicaid y el plan del conyuge son los tres caminos principales.

quickAnswer.en: Health insurance for part-time workers in 2026 comes from three main sources: (1) ACA Marketplace plans with Premium Tax Credits if your annual income is between 138% and 400% of the Federal Poverty Level ($22,025 to $63,840 for a single person), (2) Medicaid at no cost if your income is below 138% FPL ($22,025 individual) in an expansion state, or (3) a spouse's employer plan if you are married and your spouse has job-based coverage. Part-time worker benefits through an employer exist at some large companies but are not federally required for workers under 30 hours per week. COBRA is available if your hours were recently cut and you lost employer coverage, but typically costs $600 or more per month. The 2026 subsidy cliff is back: enhanced PTCs from the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 expired January 1, 2026.
quickAnswer.es: El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial en 2026 proviene de tres fuentes principales: (1) planes del Mercado ACA con Creditos Fiscales de Prima si su ingreso anual esta entre el 138% y el 400% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza ($22,025 a $63,840 para una persona), (2) Medicaid sin costo si su ingreso esta por debajo del 138% FPL en un estado de expansion, o (3) el plan del empleador del conyuge. Los beneficios de trabajador a tiempo parcial a traves de un empleador existen en algunas empresas grandes pero no son requeridos federalmente para trabajadores de menos de 30 horas semanales. El tope del subsidio 2026 ha regresado: los PTCs mejorados de la Ley de Reduccion de la Inflacion de 2022 expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026.

introParagraphs.en: Part-time workers face a coverage gap that full-time employees rarely think about. The ACA's employer mandate only requires companies with 50 or more full-time-equivalent employees to offer affordable coverage to workers averaging 30 or more hours per week. Health insurance for part-time workers is simply not required under federal law when hours fall below that threshold. If you work 25 hours a week at a restaurant, retail store, or healthcare facility, your employer has no federal obligation to offer you part-time worker benefits. That gap leaves tens of millions of hourly workers, variable-hours employees, and reduced-hours workers without job-based coverage and navigating the ACA Marketplace part-time or Medicaid on their own.
introParagraphs.es: Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial enfrentan una brecha de cobertura que los empleados de tiempo completo raramente consideran. El mandato del empleador del ACA solo exige a las empresas con 50 o mas empleados equivalentes de tiempo completo que ofrezcan cobertura asequible a trabajadores que promedian 30 o mas horas semanales. El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial simplemente no es requerido por la ley federal cuando las horas caen por debajo de ese umbral. Esa brecha deja a decenas de millones de trabajadores por horas sin cobertura basada en el empleo y navegando el Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial o Medicaid por su cuenta.

introParagraphs.en: Part-time employee health coverage depends heavily on income level. Workers earning under $22,025 a year (138% of the 2026 Federal Poverty Level for a single person) likely qualify for Medicaid at no premium cost in the 40 states that have expanded Medicaid under the ACA. Check [whether your state expanded Medicaid](/en/qa/state-medicaid-expansion-status) before assuming eligibility. Medicaid part-time income eligibility means your gross household income from all sources, including multiple part-time jobs, counts toward the threshold. Workers earning between $22,025 and $63,840 a year qualify for ACA Marketplace premium subsidies — see [who qualifies for an ACA subsidy](/en/qa/who-qualifies-for-aca-subsidy) for the full income table. The 2026 subsidy cliff is back at 400% FPL: above $63,840 single, you pay the full unsubsidized premium. ACA marketplace part-time enrollment is available during open enrollment (November 1 through January 15 for most states) or within 60 days of a qualifying life event.
introParagraphs.es: La cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial depende en gran medida del nivel de ingresos. Los trabajadores que ganan menos de $22,025 al ano (138% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza 2026 para una persona) probablemente califican para Medicaid sin costo de prima en los 40 estados que han expandido Medicaid. Los trabajadores que ganan entre $22,025 y $63,840 al ano califican para subsidios de prima en el Mercado ACA. El tope del subsidio 2026 ha regresado al 400% FPL: los PTCs mejorados de la Ley de Reduccion de la Inflacion de 2022 expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026.

footnote.es: Costos mostrados para el ano del plan 2026. El tope del subsidio del 400% FPL regreso el 1 de enero de 2026. La disponibilidad de Medicaid depende del estado de expansion de su estado.

heading.en: Option 1: ACA Marketplace with Premium Tax Credits
heading.es: Opcion 1: Mercado ACA con Credito Fiscal de Prima

paragraphs.en: ACA marketplace part-time enrollment is the most common path for workers who lack employer coverage and earn between 138% and 400% FPL. If your projected 2026 Modified Adjusted Gross Income falls in that range, you qualify for a Premium Tax Credit (PTC) that lowers your monthly premium. For a single part-time employee, that income window is $22,025 to $63,840 in 2026. For a family of four, it runs $45,540 to $132,000. Bronze plans often cost $0 to $50 a month for low-income hourly workers after the credit is applied. Silver plans, which offer cost-sharing reductions at incomes below 250% FPL, are the most valuable tier for workers in the $22,025 to $39,900 range. Part-time employee health coverage through the marketplace is comprehensive: it covers all 10 ACA essential health benefits including doctor visits, hospitalizations, mental health, and prescriptions.
paragraphs.es: La inscripcion en el Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial es el camino mas comun para los trabajadores que carecen de cobertura del empleador y ganan entre el 138% y el 400% del FPL. Si su Ingreso Bruto Ajustado Modificado proyectado para 2026 cae en ese rango, califica para un Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) que reduce su prima mensual. Para un empleado a tiempo parcial individual, esa ventana de ingresos es de $22,025 a $63,840 en 2026. La cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial a traves del mercado es integral: cubre los 10 beneficios esenciales de salud del ACA.

paragraphs.en: Variable-hours employees face an income-projection challenge that the marketplace cannot solve for you automatically. The marketplace gives advance credits each month based on the annual income estimate you submit. If your hours drop mid-year and your income falls, update your marketplace account within 30 days so your credits increase. If your income comes in higher than estimated at tax time, you repay the excess credit on Form 8962 attached to your federal return, along with the Section 1095-A form the marketplace sends each January. Workers with multiple part-time jobs should add all income streams when estimating MAGI. Medicaid part-time income thresholds count gross household income from all sources, and the same principle applies on the marketplace side: all income counts before deductions when estimating PTC eligibility.
paragraphs.es: Los empleados con horario variable enfrentan un desafio de proyeccion de ingresos. El mercado otorga creditos por adelantado cada mes basados en el estimado de ingresos que usted presenta. Si sus horas caen a mitad de ano, actualice su cuenta del mercado dentro de 30 dias. Si sus ingresos resultan mayores al estimado al momento de declarar impuestos, reembolsa el credito excedente en el Formulario 8962 junto con el Formulario 1095-A que el mercado envia cada enero.

heading.en: Option 2: Medicaid for Low-Income Part-Time Workers
heading.es: Opcion 2: Medicaid para Trabajadores a Tiempo Parcial de Bajos Ingresos

paragraphs.en: Medicaid part-time income eligibility covers workers earning under 138% of the 2026 FPL in states that have expanded Medicaid under the ACA. For a single person, that is $22,025 in 2026. For a family of four, it is $45,540. Medicaid covers the same essential health benefits as marketplace plans: doctor visits, hospital care, preventive services, mental health, and prescription drugs, at zero or minimal out-of-pocket cost. For a part-time retail or food-service worker earning $18,000 a year in an expansion state, Medicaid is almost always the right first call. Health insurance for part-time workers through Medicaid has no open enrollment deadline: you can apply any day of the year and coverage can start as soon as the month you apply in many states.
paragraphs.es: La elegibilidad de Medicaid por ingresos a tiempo parcial cubre a los trabajadores que ganan menos del 138% del FPL 2026 en estados que han expandido Medicaid. Para una persona, eso es $22,025 en 2026. Para una familia de cuatro, es $45,540. El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial a traves de Medicaid no tiene fecha limite de inscripcion abierta: puede solicitar cualquier dia del ano y la cobertura puede comenzar tan pronto como el mes de la solicitud en muchos estados.

paragraphs.en: Non-expansion states (including Texas, Florida, Georgia, and roughly 10 others as of 2026) do not cover childless adults under Medicaid at 138% FPL. Part-time employees in those states who earn between the state's Medicaid limit and 100% FPL ($15,960 single in 2026) fall into the ACA coverage gap: they earn too much for state Medicaid but too little to qualify for marketplace subsidies, which start at 100% FPL. Part-time worker benefits through a state marketplace or safety-net program may still be available in non-expansion states. The screener at CoveredUSA can identify state-specific programs. Workers who move from a non-expansion state to an expansion state trigger a Marketplace Special Enrollment Period and may become Medicaid-eligible immediately.
paragraphs.es: Los estados sin expansion (incluyendo Texas, Florida, Georgia y alrededor de 10 mas al 2026) no cubren adultos sin dependientes bajo Medicaid al 138% FPL. Los empleados a tiempo parcial en esos estados que ganan entre el limite de Medicaid estatal y el 100% FPL ($15,960 individual en 2026) caen en la brecha de cobertura del ACA. Los beneficios de trabajador a tiempo parcial a traves de un programa estatal de seguridad pueden seguir disponibles en estados sin expansion.

heading.es: Opcion 3: Plan del Empleador del Conyuge

paragraphs.en: Part-time employee health coverage through a spouse's employer plan is often the most cost-effective option for married workers. Employer-sponsored plans are paid pretax through payroll, which is typically cheaper than an equivalent marketplace plan even after accounting for the employee share of the premium. Part-time workers married to a full-time employee with employer coverage can join that plan during the spouse's open enrollment or within 60 days of a qualifying life event such as marriage, a new child, or loss of other coverage. One important rule: if a spouse's employer plan is considered affordable under ACA rules and you decline it to buy on the marketplace instead, you generally lose eligibility for Premium Tax Credits. The IRS treats affordable employer coverage offered to a family member as disqualifying for the person covered, even if that person is only a part-time worker.
paragraphs.es: La cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial a traves del plan del empleador del conyuge es a menudo la opcion mas rentable para los trabajadores casados. Los planes del empleador se pagan antes de impuestos a traves de la nomina. Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial casados con un empleado de tiempo completo con cobertura del empleador pueden unirse a ese plan durante la inscripcion abierta del conyuge o dentro de los 60 dias de un evento calificador de vida. Una regla importante: si declina el plan del conyuge para comprar en el mercado, generalmente pierde la elegibilidad para el Credito Fiscal de Prima.

paragraphs.en: Variable-hours workers and reduced-hours employees who previously had employer-sponsored coverage and lost it because their hours were cut can elect COBRA continuation coverage. COBRA lets you stay on your former employer's plan for up to 18 months, but you now pay the full premium (both the employer's share and your own share) plus a 2% administrative fee. For most part-time workers, COBRA runs $600 to $1,800 per month and is far more expensive than a subsidized ACA marketplace plan. ACA marketplace part-time plans almost always cost less than COBRA for workers who qualify for the Premium Tax Credit. COBRA makes sense only when you are mid-treatment with in-network providers that a new marketplace plan would not cover, or when the COBRA plan's prescription formulary includes a critical medication that marketplace plans in your area exclude. Losing employer coverage, including an hours-reduction event, triggers a 60-day Special Enrollment Period on the ACA Marketplace.
paragraphs.es: Los trabajadores con horario variable y empleados con horas reducidas que tenian cobertura del empleador y la perdieron porque sus horas se redujeron pueden elegir la cobertura de continuacion COBRA. COBRA le permite permanecer en el plan de su empleador hasta 18 meses, pero ahora paga la prima completa mas una tarifa administrativa del 2%. Para la mayoria de los trabajadores a tiempo parcial, los planes del Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial cuestan casi siempre menos que COBRA para quienes califican para el Credito Fiscal de Prima. COBRA tiene sentido solo cuando esta en medio de un tratamiento con proveedores en la red que un nuevo plan del mercado no cubriria.

intro.en: Part-time employees and hourly workers are frequently targeted by non-ACA products marketed as affordable alternatives. These are the products to avoid:
intro.es: Los empleados a tiempo parcial y trabajadores por horas son frecuentemente blanco de productos no-ACA comercializados como alternativas asequibles. Estos son los productos que debe evitar:

es: Trampa
Por que evitarla

en: Mini-med or limited-benefit plans from your employer

Some employers offer part-time workers fixed-dollar plans that cap total coverage at $5,000 to $25,000 per year. A single ER visit or hospitalization can exceed the cap. These are not ACA-compliant and do not protect you from large medical bills.
es: Planes mini-med o de beneficios limitados de su empleador

Algunos empleadores ofrecen a trabajadores a tiempo parcial planes de dolares fijos que limitan la cobertura total a $5,000 a $25,000 por ano. No cumplen el ACA y no lo protegen de facturas medicas grandes.

en: Do not have to cover pre-existing conditions, can rescind coverage after the fact, and do not count as minimum essential coverage. A cancer diagnosis or surgery can leave you with bills the short-term plan refuses to pay.
es: Planes de salud a corto plazo de duracion limitada

No tienen que cubrir condiciones preexistentes, pueden cancelar la cobertura retroactivamente y no cuentan como cobertura esencial minima. Un diagnostico de cancer o una cirugia puede dejarle con facturas que el plan a corto plazo se niega a pagar.

en: Health share ministries (Medi-Share, Sedera, Liberty HealthShare)

NOT insurance. No legal obligation to pay any claim. Pre-existing conditions, mental health, maternity, and substance use treatment are routinely excluded. Part-time workers with variable income cannot rely on an organization with no claims obligation.
es: Ministerios de compartir salud (Medi-Share, Sedera, Liberty HealthShare)
NO son seguro. No tienen obligacion legal de pagar ningun reclamo. Las condiciones preexistentes, salud mental, maternidad y tratamiento de adicciones son rutinariamente excluidos.

en: Waiting too long after losing employer coverage

COBRA and the Marketplace SEP both have a 60-day window from the date you lose coverage. Miss that window and you must wait until open enrollment (November 1 through January 15 for most states). Part-time employees whose hours are cut should file a marketplace SEP application immediately.
es: Esperar demasiado despues de perder la cobertura del empleador

COBRA y el PEI del Mercado tienen una ventana de 60 dias desde la fecha en que pierde la cobertura. Los empleados a tiempo parcial cuyas horas se reducen deben solicitar un PEI del mercado de inmediato.

footnote.en: Verify any plan is ACA-compliant (covers all 10 essential health benefits) before enrolling. Only plans sold on HealthCare.gov or a state marketplace are guaranteed to be ACA-compliant.
footnote.es: Verifique que cualquier plan cumpla el ACA (cubre los 10 beneficios esenciales de salud) antes de inscribirse. Solo los planes vendidos en HealthCare.gov o un mercado estatal estan garantizados como cumpliendo el ACA.

heading.en: Premium Tax Credit (PTC) eligibility for part-time workers in 2026
heading.es: Elegibilidad al Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC) para trabajadores a tiempo parcial en 2026

paragraphs.en: Part-time workers projecting their 2026 household income need to know one number: 400% of the Federal Poverty Level. For a single part-time employee, that is $63,840 in 2026. For a household of four, it is $132,000. Below that line, the Premium Tax Credit phases down as income climbs. Subsidies do not snap off at 250% or 300% FPL: they get progressively smaller. At 400% FPL they stop entirely. Variable-hours employees and hourly workers near the 350% to 400% FPL range ($55,860 to $63,840 single in 2026) face the steepest phase-down and should model projected income carefully. The 2026 ACA Marketplace out-of-pocket maximum is $10,600 for an individual plan, which is the ceiling on what any marketplace plan can require you to pay in a year. Part-time worker benefits from the PTC are delivered as advance credits that reduce your monthly premium, then reconciled at tax filing using the Section 1095-A the marketplace sends each January.
paragraphs.es: Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial que proyectan sus ingresos 2026 necesitan conocer un numero: 400% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza. Para un empleado a tiempo parcial individual, eso es $63,840 en 2026. Para un hogar de cuatro, es $132,000. Por debajo de esa linea, el Credito Fiscal de Prima disminuye progresivamente a medida que aumentan los ingresos. Al 400% FPL se detienen por completo. Los beneficios de trabajador a tiempo parcial del PTC se entregan como creditos anticipados que reducen su prima mensual, luego se reconcilian al declarar impuestos usando el Formulario 1095-A.

paragraphs.en: ACA marketplace part-time enrollees who also work a second job, do gig work, or receive freelance income must include all income sources when projecting MAGI. Medicaid part-time income rules count gross household income from all sources before deductions. The same principle applies on the marketplace side: wages from multiple part-time jobs, tips, 1099 income, and investment income all count. If combined income from all sources crosses 400% FPL, you pay the full unsubsidized premium. Reduced-hours employees who experience a mid-year income drop (fewer hours scheduled, seasonal slowdowns, or a second job ending) should update their marketplace income estimate immediately. The marketplace will increase advance credits going forward and you reconcile the full year on Form 8962 with your federal return.
paragraphs.es: Los inscritos en el Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial que tambien trabajan un segundo empleo, hacen trabajo gig o reciben ingresos freelance deben incluir todas las fuentes de ingreso al proyectar el MAGI. Las reglas de ingresos a tiempo parcial de Medicaid cuentan los ingresos brutos del hogar de todas las fuentes antes de deducciones. El mismo principio aplica en el lado del mercado: los salarios de multiples empleos a tiempo parcial, propinas, ingresos 1099 e ingresos de inversiones cuentan todos.

list.en: Below 138% FPL ($22,025 single / $45,540 family of four in 2026): Medicaid in expansion states, no marketplace subsidy needed
list.es: Por debajo del 138% FPL ($22,025 individual / $45,540 familia de cuatro en 2026): Medicaid en estados de expansion

list.en: 138% to 250% FPL ($22,025 to $39,900 single in 2026): PTC plus Silver plan cost-sharing reductions (CSRs)
list.es: 138% a 250% FPL ($22,025 a $39,900 individual en 2026): PTC mas reducciones de costos compartidos (CSR) en planes Silver

list.en: 250% to 400% FPL ($39,900 to $63,840 single in 2026): PTC only, no cost-sharing reductions
list.es: 250% a 400% FPL ($39,900 a $63,840 individual en 2026): PTC solamente, sin reducciones de costos compartidos

list.en: Above 400% FPL ($63,840+ single in 2026): no PTC, full unsubsidized premium
list.es: Por encima del 400% FPL ($63,840+ individual en 2026): sin PTC, prima completa sin subsidio

heading.en: Household size and 2026 income thresholds for part-time workers
heading.es: Tamano del hogar y umbrales de ingresos 2026 para trabajadores a tiempo parcial

paragraphs.en: Medicaid expansion states cover adults earning below 138% of the 2026 Federal Poverty Level. The ACA Marketplace Premium Tax Credit phases down between 138% FPL and 400% FPL. Health insurance for part-time workers depends on which bracket your household falls into. Part-time employee health coverage options shift substantially based on household size: a family of four earning $45,000 qualifies for Medicaid in an expansion state, while the same income for a single person puts them in the PTC-subsidy range. Medicaid part-time income eligibility is year-round: you can apply any day. ACA marketplace part-time coverage requires enrollment during open enrollment or within 60 days of a qualifying event.
paragraphs.es: Los estados de expansion de Medicaid cubren adultos que ganan por debajo del 138% del Nivel Federal de Pobreza 2026. El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial depende del rango en el que cae su hogar. La cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial cambia sustancialmente segun el tamano del hogar. La elegibilidad por ingresos a tiempo parcial de Medicaid es durante todo el ano: puede solicitar cualquier dia.

caption.en: 2026 Income Thresholds for Part-Time Workers: Medicaid (138% FPL) and ACA Subsidy Cliff (400% FPL)
caption.es: Umbrales de Ingresos 2026 para Trabajadores a Tiempo Parcial: Medicaid (138% FPL) y Tope de Subsidio ACA (400% FPL)

es: Tamano del hogar
138% FPL (expansion Medicaid, 2026)
400% FPL (tope subsidio, 2026)
Cada persona adicional

footnote.en: 2026 FPL base: $15,960 (single person, 48 contiguous states and DC). Medicaid 138% column applies to expansion states only; non-expansion states use lower state-specific thresholds. 400% FPL column reflects the ACA Marketplace subsidy cliff in effect January 1, 2026 (enhanced PTCs from ARPA/IRA expired).
footnote.es: FPL base 2026: $15,960 (persona individual, 48 estados contiguos y DC). La columna del 138% de Medicaid aplica solo en estados de expansion. La columna del 400% FPL refleja el tope del subsidio del Mercado ACA vigente desde el 1 de enero de 2026.

heading.en: HSA and HDHP fit for part-time workers in 2026
heading.es: HSA y HDHP para trabajadores a tiempo parcial en 2026

paragraphs.en: Part-time employees and hourly workers who enroll in an ACA Marketplace plan can open a Health Savings Account (HSA) only if their plan qualifies as a High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). For 2026, an HDHP must have a minimum deductible of $1,700 for self-only coverage or $3,400 for family coverage, and a maximum out-of-pocket of $8,500 self-only or $17,000 family (IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19). The 2026 HSA contribution limit is $4,400 for self-only coverage or $8,750 for family coverage, plus a $1,000 catch-up if you are 55 or older. The triple tax advantage (tax-deductible contributions, tax-free growth, tax-free qualified withdrawals) is available to any part-time worker enrolled in a qualifying HDHP. Health insurance for part-time workers paired with an HSA often delivers the lowest effective annual cost for healthy workers who do not use much care, because the lower HDHP premium plus the HSA tax savings outweigh the higher deductible.
paragraphs.es: Los empleados a tiempo parcial y trabajadores por horas que se inscriben en un plan del Mercado ACA pueden abrir una Cuenta de Ahorros para la Salud (HSA) solo si su plan califica como Plan de Deducible Alto (HDHP). Para 2026, un HDHP debe tener un deducible minimo de $1,700 individual o $3,400 familiar, y un maximo de bolsillo de $8,500 individual o $17,000 familiar. El limite de contribucion HSA 2026 es $4,400 individual o $8,750 familiar, mas $1,000 adicional a los 55+. El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial combinado con una HSA a menudo entrega el menor costo anual efectivo para trabajadores saludables.

paragraphs.en: A Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is entirely different from an HSA. FSAs are offered only through employers as a payroll benefit. Most part-time employees do not have access to a Flexible Spending Account because their employer either does not offer it or restricts it to full-time employees. Part-time worker benefits through an FSA, when available, can pay for medical copays, prescriptions, and dental expenses tax-free. HSA dollars roll over year to year and are fully portable: a reduced-hours employee who loses their HDHP enrollment must stop contributing but can continue spending the balance on qualified medical expenses at any time. Non-HDHP marketplace plans (Bronze, Silver, Gold, and Platinum plans that do not meet the 2026 HDHP deductible minimum) do not qualify for HSA contributions.
paragraphs.es: Una Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles (FSA) es completamente diferente de una HSA. Las FSA se ofrecen solo a traves de empleadores como beneficio de nomina. La mayoria de los empleados a tiempo parcial no tienen acceso a una Cuenta de Gastos Flexibles. Los beneficios de trabajador a tiempo parcial a traves de una FSA, cuando estan disponibles, pueden pagar copagos medicos, recetas y gastos dentales libres de impuestos. Los dolares de la HSA se acumulan ano tras ano y son totalmente portables.

heading.en: Tax deductions for part-time workers: Form 7206 and what applies
heading.es: Deducciones fiscales para trabajadores a tiempo parcial: Formulario 7206 y que aplica

paragraphs.en: Form 7206 (the self-employed health insurance deduction) does not apply to part-time workers who receive W-2 wages. Form 7206 is exclusively for people with net self-employment earnings: Schedule C or Schedule F filers, partners in a partnership, or S-corporation shareholders who pay themselves wages. Part-time employees receiving only W-2 wages cannot use Form 7206. This is true even if you work for multiple employers part-time: receiving multiple W-2 forms does not create self-employment income. If you have both a W-2 part-time job and a side business with net self-employment profit reported on Schedule C, the Form 7206 deduction applies only to the self-employment portion of your income. Critically, Form 7206 reduces income tax only: it does NOT reduce self-employment tax on Schedule SE. The 15.3% self-employment tax is calculated on Schedule SE, which excludes the health insurance deduction entirely.
paragraphs.es: El Formulario 7206 (la deduccion de seguro medico para trabajadores independientes) no aplica a los trabajadores a tiempo parcial que reciben salarios W-2. El Formulario 7206 es exclusivamente para personas con ingresos netos de trabajo independiente: declarantes del Schedule C o F, socios en una sociedad, o accionistas de S-corporation. Criticamente, el Formulario 7206 reduce solo el impuesto sobre la renta: NO reduce el impuesto de trabajo independiente en el Schedule SE.

paragraphs.en: Part-time employees who pay their own marketplace premiums may be able to deduct the premiums as a medical expense on Schedule A if they itemize deductions. The Schedule A medical expense deduction applies only to the portion of total medical expenses that exceeds 7.5% of your Adjusted Gross Income (AGI). For most hourly workers and reduced-hours employees, the 2026 standard deduction ($14,600 single, $29,200 married filing jointly) exceeds any itemized deductions, making the Schedule A medical deduction impractical. The most effective tax tool for most part-time employee health coverage situations is contributing to an HSA (when enrolled in an HDHP), since HSA contributions reduce AGI directly via Schedule 1 on Form 1040. ACA marketplace part-time enrollees who receive advance PTCs must file Form 8962 and attach Section 1095-A at tax time to reconcile the credits.
paragraphs.es: Los empleados a tiempo parcial que pagan sus propias primas del mercado pueden deducir las primas como gasto medico en el Schedule A si declaran deducciones detalladas. Para la mayoria de los trabajadores por horas, la deduccion estandar 2026 ($14,600 individual) supera cualquier deduccion detallada. La herramienta fiscal mas efectiva para la mayoria de situaciones de cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial es contribuir a una HSA si estan inscritos en un HDHP.

heading.en: Marketplace Special Enrollment Period (SEP) triggers for part-time workers
heading.es: Disparadores del Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (PEI) del Mercado para trabajadores a tiempo parcial

paragraphs.en: Part-time employees and hourly workers encounter several qualifying life events that open a 60-day [Special Enrollment Period](/en/glossary/special-enrollment-period) on the ACA Marketplace outside of the annual open enrollment window (November 1 through January 15 for most states). The 60-day window starts on the date of the qualifying event. ACA marketplace part-time enrollment through a SEP is frequently the fastest way for a variable-hours worker to get covered when job-based insurance disappears. Missing the 60-day SEP window means waiting until the next open enrollment period, which could leave you without part-time employee health coverage for months.
paragraphs.es: Los empleados a tiempo parcial y trabajadores por horas encuentran varios eventos calificadores de vida que abren un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (PEI) de 60 dias en el Mercado ACA. La inscripcion en el Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial a traves de un PEI es frecuentemente la forma mas rapida para un trabajador con horario variable de obtener cobertura cuando desaparece el seguro basado en el empleo. Perderse la ventana de 60 dias del PEI significa esperar hasta el proximo periodo de inscripcion abierta.

paragraphs.en: Part-time workers whose income drops mid-year to below 138% FPL should apply for Medicaid immediately rather than wait for the next marketplace open enrollment. Medicaid part-time income rules allow year-round enrollment and coverage can start as soon as the month of application in many expansion states. If a part-time employee's income later rises back above 138% FPL, losing Medicaid eligibility is itself a qualifying event that opens a 60-day Marketplace SEP. Health insurance for part-time workers changes as income fluctuates: the system is designed to have a coverage path at every income level, but you must actively update your enrollment when your situation changes.
paragraphs.es: Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial cuyo ingreso baja a mitad de ano por debajo del 138% FPL deben solicitar Medicaid de inmediato. Las reglas de ingresos a tiempo parcial de Medicaid permiten la inscripcion durante todo el ano. El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial cambia a medida que fluctuan los ingresos: el sistema esta disenado para tener un camino de cobertura en cada nivel de ingresos, pero debe actualizar activamente su inscripcion cuando cambia su situacion.

list.en: Loss of employer coverage (including hours cut below the plan threshold): 60-day SEP window from the date coverage ends
list.es: Perdida de cobertura del empleador (incluyendo horas reducidas por debajo del umbral): ventana de PEI de 60 dias desde que termina la cobertura

list.en: Loss of Medicaid or CHIP eligibility (income rises above 138% FPL): 60-day SEP
list.es: Perdida de elegibilidad para Medicaid o CHIP (ingreso sube por encima del 138% FPL): PEI de 60 dias

list.en: Marriage or entering a domestic partnership: 60-day SEP
list.es: Matrimonio o ingreso a una sociedad domestica: PEI de 60 dias

list.en: Divorce or legal separation causing loss of coverage: 60-day SEP
list.es: Divorcio o separacion legal que cause perdida de cobertura: PEI de 60 dias

list.en: Birth or adoption of a child: 60-day SEP
list.es: Nacimiento o adopcion de un hijo: PEI de 60 dias

list.en: Permanent move to a new state or to a new coverage area: 60-day SEP
list.es: Mudanza permanente a un nuevo estado o nueva area de cobertura: PEI de 60 dias

list.en: Turning 26 and aging off a parent's plan: 60-day SEP
list.es: Cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres: PEI de 60 dias

heading.en: How to enroll in health coverage as a part-time worker in 2026
heading.es: Como inscribirse en cobertura de salud como trabajador a tiempo parcial en 2026

paragraphs.en: Part-time employees and hourly workers who need health insurance for part-time workers should start at HealthCare.gov (or their state marketplace) to check whether they qualify for Medicaid, a subsidized marketplace plan, or CHIP for children. The application takes 30 to 45 minutes and delivers an eligibility determination on the spot. Reduced-hours employees applying under a Special Enrollment Period must have documentation of the qualifying event: a letter from the employer stating the coverage end date, a COBRA election notice, or proof of the life event. Documents typically needed include proof of income (pay stubs, W-2, or prior-year tax return), proof of household size, a government-issued ID, and Social Security numbers for all household members.
paragraphs.es: Los empleados a tiempo parcial y trabajadores por horas que necesitan seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial deben comenzar en HealthCare.gov (o el mercado de su estado) para verificar si califican para Medicaid, un plan subsidiado del mercado o CHIP para ninos. La solicitud toma 30 a 45 minutos y proporciona una determinacion de elegibilidad de inmediato. Los empleados con horas reducidas que solicitan bajo un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial deben tener documentacion del evento calificador.

list.en: Step 1: Go to HealthCare.gov and create or log into your account. If your state runs its own marketplace (California, New York, Colorado, Washington, and others), go to the state marketplace directly.
list.es: Paso 1: Vaya a HealthCare.gov y cree o inicie sesion en su cuenta. Si su estado tiene su propio mercado (California, Nueva York, Colorado, Washington y otros), vaya directamente al mercado estatal.

list.en: Step 2: Enter your household size and projected 2026 income. For hourly workers and variable-hours employees, use your best estimate for the full year based on current hours and hourly rate. Include income from all part-time jobs.
list.es: Paso 2: Ingrese el tamano de su hogar y el ingreso proyectado para 2026. Para trabajadores por horas y empleados con horario variable, use su mejor estimado para el ano completo. Incluya ingresos de todos los empleos a tiempo parcial.

list.en: Step 3: Review your eligibility result. If Medicaid is offered, you can enroll immediately at any time of year. If a subsidized marketplace plan is offered, choose a plan and confirm enrollment before the monthly deadline (typically the 15th for coverage starting the 1st of the following month).
list.es: Paso 3: Revise su resultado de elegibilidad. Si se ofrece Medicaid, puede inscribirse de inmediato en cualquier momento del ano. Si se ofrece un plan subsidiado del mercado, elija un plan y confirme la inscripcion antes de la fecha limite mensual.

list.en: Step 4: Pay your first premium (if enrolling in a marketplace plan) by the plan's due date to activate coverage. Medicaid has no premium in most expansion states.
list.es: Paso 4: Pague su primera prima (si se inscribe en un plan del mercado) antes de la fecha limite del plan para activar la cobertura. Medicaid no tiene prima en la mayoria de los estados de expansion.

list.en: Step 5: Save your Section 1095-A form from the marketplace. You will need it to file Form 8962 and reconcile your advance Premium Tax Credits when you file your federal return.
list.es: Paso 5: Guarde su Formulario 1095-A del mercado. Lo necesitara para presentar el Formulario 8962 y reconciliar sus Creditos Fiscales de Prima anticipados al declarar impuestos.

en.question: What is the cheapest health insurance for part-time workers in 2026?
en.answer: Health insurance for part-time workers costs the least through Medicaid (free or near-free) for those earning under $22,025 a year single in an expansion state. For workers earning between $22,025 and $63,840, a subsidized Bronze or Silver plan on the ACA Marketplace is typically $0 to $200 per month after the Premium Tax Credit applies. Part-time workers with a spouse who has employer coverage can often join that plan at the lowest total household cost. COBRA is the most expensive option at $600 to $1,800 per month and should generally be the last resort.

en.question: Do part-time workers qualify for the Premium Tax Credit (PTC)?
en.answer: Yes, if your 2026 household income is between 100% and 400% FPL and you are not eligible for affordable employer coverage, Medicaid, or Medicare. For a single part-time employee in 2026, that means earning between $15,960 and $63,840. The 2026 subsidy cliff is back: enhanced PTCs from the 2022 Inflation Reduction Act expired January 1, 2026, so subsidies phase down approaching 400% FPL and stop entirely at $63,840 (single). ACA marketplace part-time enrollees at multiple jobs should add all income sources when projecting eligibility for the PTC.

en.question: Are employers required to offer health insurance to part-time workers?
en.answer: Not under federal law when workers average under 30 hours per week. The ACA employer mandate requires businesses with 50 or more full-time-equivalent employees to offer affordable coverage to workers averaging 30 or more hours per week. Health insurance for part-time workers below that threshold is entirely voluntary for employers. Some states have additional requirements and some large employers voluntarily extend part-time worker benefits to hourly employees, but there is no federal mandate for workers under 30 hours.

en.question: Can part-time workers use an HSA?
en.answer: Yes, if you enroll in an HSA-qualified High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). For 2026, an HDHP has a minimum deductible of $1,700 (self-only) or $3,400 (family) per IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-19. The 2026 HSA contribution limit is $4,400 self-only or $8,750 family, plus $1,000 catch-up at age 55 or older. The triple tax advantage: contributions reduce your taxable income, growth is tax-free, and qualified medical withdrawals are tax-free. Part-time employee health coverage through a non-HDHP plan does not qualify for HSA contributions. A Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is employer-only: most part-time workers do not have FSA access.

en.question: Can part-time workers deduct health insurance premiums on taxes?
en.answer: W-2 part-time employees cannot use Form 7206, which is the self-employed health insurance deduction. Form 7206 applies only to people with net self-employment income on Schedule C. Part-time employees may deduct marketplace premiums on Schedule A as an itemized medical expense, but only the amount above 7.5% of AGI counts, and the 2026 standard deduction ($14,600 single) usually makes itemizing unproductive. The most practical tax tool for most part-time worker benefits situations is contributing to an HSA (if on an HDHP), since HSA contributions reduce adjusted gross income directly via Schedule 1.

en.question: What happens when part-time workers lose employer coverage because hours are cut?
en.answer: Losing employer coverage because your hours drop below the plan threshold is a qualifying life event that opens a 60-day Marketplace Special Enrollment Period. During that window, you can enroll in ACA marketplace part-time plans at any time without waiting for open enrollment. You can also elect COBRA for up to 18 months, but COBRA typically costs $600 to $1,800 per month at full premium plus a 2% fee. Most variable-hours workers find a subsidized marketplace plan far cheaper than COBRA. Apply for the Marketplace SEP as soon as you receive written confirmation of your coverage end date.

en.question: What if a part-time worker earns too much for Medicaid but wants low-cost coverage?
en.answer: Part-time employee health coverage is available through the ACA Marketplace at subsidized rates for workers earning between 138% and 400% FPL ($22,025 to $63,840 single in 2026). Workers earning above 400% FPL pay the full unsubsidized marketplace premium. Medicaid part-time income eligibility stops at 138% FPL in expansion states: above that level, the marketplace is the primary path. At higher incomes, enrolling in an HSA-qualified HDHP typically delivers the lowest effective cost: the lower premium plus the HSA triple tax advantage reduces your real out-of-pocket spend compared to a higher-metal-tier plan.

en.question: When can part-time workers enroll in a Marketplace plan outside open enrollment?
en.answer: Part-time employees qualify for a 60-day Marketplace SEP after these events: losing employer coverage (including hours cut below the plan threshold), losing Medicaid eligibility, getting married or divorced, having a child, moving to a new coverage area, or turning 26 and aging off a parent's plan. ACA marketplace part-time enrollment during a SEP must begin within 60 days of the qualifying event. Medicaid has no enrollment deadline and accepts applications year-round. After any qualifying event, part-time workers should apply immediately rather than waiting for the annual open enrollment window (November 1 through January 15 for most states).

es.question: ?Cual es el seguro medico mas barato para trabajadores a tiempo parcial en 2026?
es.answer: El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial cuesta menos a traves de Medicaid (gratis o casi gratis) para quienes ganan menos de $22,025 al ano (individual) en un estado de expansion. Para trabajadores que ganan entre $22,025 y $63,840, un plan Bronze o Silver subsidiado en el Mercado ACA es tipicamente $0 a $200 por mes despues de aplicar el Credito Fiscal de Prima. Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial con un conyuge que tiene cobertura del empleador a menudo pueden unirse a ese plan al menor costo total del hogar.

es.question: ?Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial califican para el Credito Fiscal de Prima (PTC)?
es.answer: Si, si el ingreso de su hogar en 2026 esta entre el 100% y el 400% del FPL y no es elegible para cobertura asequible del empleador, Medicaid o Medicare. Para un empleado a tiempo parcial individual en 2026, eso significa ganar entre $15,960 y $63,840. El tope del subsidio 2026 ha regresado: los creditos mejorados de la Ley de Reduccion de la Inflacion de 2022 expiraron el 1 de enero de 2026. Los inscritos en el Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial con multiples empleos deben sumar todas las fuentes de ingresos al proyectar la elegibilidad.

es.question: ?Los empleadores estan obligados a ofrecer seguro medico a empleados a tiempo parcial?
es.answer: No bajo la ley federal si trabaja menos de 30 horas semanales. El mandato del empleador del ACA requiere que empresas con 50 o mas empleados de tiempo completo equivalente ofrezcan cobertura a trabajadores que promedian 30 o mas horas semanales. El seguro medico para trabajadores a tiempo parcial por debajo de ese umbral es completamente voluntario para los empleadores. Algunos estados tienen requisitos adicionales y algunos grandes empleadores voluntariamente extienden beneficios a empleados por horas.

es.question: ?Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial pueden usar una HSA?
es.answer: Si, si se inscriben en un Plan de Deducible Alto (HDHP) compatible con HSA. Para 2026, un HDHP tiene un deducible minimo de $1,700 (individual) o $3,400 (familia). El limite de contribucion HSA 2026 es $4,400 individual o $8,750 familiar, mas $1,000 adicional a los 55+. La ventaja triple: las contribuciones reducen su ingreso sujeto a impuestos, el crecimiento es libre de impuestos y los retiros medicos calificados son libres de impuestos. La cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial a traves de un plan no-HDHP no califica para contribuciones HSA.

es.question: ?Los trabajadores a tiempo parcial pueden deducir las primas de seguro medico en sus impuestos?
es.answer: Los empleados W-2 a tiempo parcial no pueden usar el Formulario 7206, que es la deduccion de seguro medico para trabajadores independientes. El Formulario 7206 aplica solo a personas con ingresos netos de trabajo independiente en el Schedule C. Los empleados a tiempo parcial pueden deducir primas del mercado en el Schedule A, pero solo la cantidad por encima del 7.5% del AGI cuenta, y la deduccion estandar 2026 ($14,600 individual) generalmente hace que detallar sea improductivo. La herramienta fiscal mas practica para la mayoria de situaciones de beneficios de trabajador a tiempo parcial es contribuir a una HSA si estan en un HDHP.

es.question: ?Que sucede cuando los trabajadores a tiempo parcial pierden la cobertura del empleador porque les reducen las horas?
es.answer: Perder la cobertura del empleador porque sus horas caen por debajo del umbral del plan es un evento calificador de vida que abre un Periodo de Inscripcion Especial (PEI) del Mercado de 60 dias. Durante esa ventana, puede inscribirse en planes del Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial en cualquier momento sin esperar la inscripcion abierta. Tambien puede elegir COBRA hasta 18 meses, pero COBRA tipicamente cuesta $600 a $1,800 por mes. La mayoria de los trabajadores con horario variable encuentran un plan del mercado subsidiado mucho mas barato que COBRA.

es.question: ?Que pasa si un trabajador a tiempo parcial gana demasiado para Medicaid pero quiere cobertura de bajo costo?
es.answer: La cobertura medica de empleados a tiempo parcial esta disponible en el Mercado ACA a tarifas subsidiadas para trabajadores que ganan entre el 138% y el 400% FPL ($22,025 a $63,840 individual en 2026). La elegibilidad por ingresos a tiempo parcial de Medicaid se detiene al 138% FPL en estados de expansion. Por encima del 400% FPL, inscribirse en un HDHP compatible con HSA tipicamente entrega el menor costo efectivo: la prima mas baja mas la ventaja triple del HSA reduce su gasto real en comparacion con un plan de metal mas alto.

es.question: ?Cuando pueden inscribirse los trabajadores a tiempo parcial en un plan del Mercado fuera de la inscripcion abierta?
es.answer: Los empleados a tiempo parcial califican para un PEI del Mercado de 60 dias despues de estos eventos: perder la cobertura del empleador, perder la elegibilidad para Medicaid, casarse o divorciarse, tener un hijo, mudarse a una nueva area de cobertura, o cumplir 26 anos y salir del plan de los padres. La inscripcion en el Mercado ACA a tiempo parcial durante un PEI debe comenzar dentro de los 60 dias del evento calificador. Medicaid no tiene fecha limite de inscripcion y acepta solicitudes durante todo el ano.
